name: Write and Run Your First Playbook
description: Create a basic Ansible playbook to install packages, configure services, and deploy changes across multiple hosts.
trendingReason: Ansible remains the most popular agentless automation tool for infrastructure configuration.

steps.title: Create a playbook file
steps.command:
cat > site.yml << 'EOF'
---
- name: Configure web servers
  hosts: webservers
  become: true
  tasks:
    - name: Install nginx
      apt:
        name: nginx
        state: present
        update_cache: true

    - name: Start nginx service
      service:
        name: nginx
        state: started
        enabled: true
EOF
steps.description: Creates a YAML playbook that installs and starts nginx on all hosts in the webservers group.
steps.tip: Always use 'become: true' when tasks require root privileges.

steps.title: Run the playbook in check mode
steps.description: Performs a dry run showing what changes would be made without actually applying them.
steps.tip: Always run --check first on production systems to preview changes safely.

steps.title: Run the playbook
steps.description: Executes the playbook against all hosts defined in the webservers group.

steps.title: Run with verbose output
steps.description: Shows detailed task output. Use -vv or -vvv for even more debugging information.

steps.title: Limit to specific hosts
steps.description: Runs the playbook only against a single host for targeted deployment.

fullScript:
# Write and run an Ansible playbook
cat > site.yml << 'EOF'
---
- name: Configure web servers
  hosts: webservers
  become: true
  tasks:
    - name: Install nginx
      apt:
        name: nginx
        state: present
        update_cache: true

    - name: Start nginx service
      service:
        name: nginx
        state: started
        enabled: true
EOF
ansible-playbook site.yml --check --diff
ansible-playbook site.yml

faq.question: What is the difference between a playbook and a role?
faq.answer: A playbook is a YAML file containing plays and tasks. A role is a reusable, self-contained unit of tasks, handlers, variables, and templates organized in a standard directory structure. Roles are imported into playbooks.

faq.question: How do I pass variables to a playbook?
faq.answer: Use --extra-vars 'key=value' on the command line, define them in group_vars/ or host_vars/ directories, or set them in the playbook under 'vars:' at the play or task level.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything on managed hosts?
faq.answer: No. Ansible is agentless and only requires SSH access and Python on the target hosts. Most Linux distributions include Python by default.
